note: Hand-authored idiomatic Bash lines (parameter expansion, traps, loops, tests) to give the 'code' context a shell-scripting dimension. Original MIT content within the 120-char code cap. Shares the 'code' context with code-typescript.json and code-python.json; each row carries its own source.
items: for f in *.txt; do echo "processing $f"; done
while read -r line; do echo "$line"; done < input.txt
case "$1" in start) run;; stop) halt;; *) usage;; esac
count=0; for x in "$@"; do count=$((count + 1)); done
if command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "ok"; fi
for i in "${!arr[@]}"; do echo "$i"; done
